## APRENDIZADOS-CHAVE

1. **Dollar margin beats percentage margin for DTC** — a product with 20% margin at $100 ($20 dollar margin) is far better than 40% margin at $20 ($8 dollar margin) because you need actual dollars to afford customer acquisition. ^[FIL-RW-005]

2. **Market pull is the strongest validation signal** — when people from a new market buy your product despite expensive shipping and poor delivery, that's the market pulling you in. Follow it. ^[MM-RW-001]

3. **The Second Album Problem is real** — first product gets all your attention and thought. Second product, people just throw things at the wall. This is a trap. ^[MM-RW-004]

4. **Complementary products fund acquisition** — even if you break even on the onboarding product, complementary products (zero additional CAC, zero additional shipping) are where LTV and the business model live. ^[MM-RW-012]

5. **Customer knowledge reduces CAC directly** — the closer you tell customers what they want to hear, the cheaper acquisition gets. 15,000 survey responses > 10 friends' opinions. ^[FIL-RW-013, HEUR-RW-005]

6. **Bad reviews are business intelligence** — not from bad people, but good people with bad experiences. They reveal delivery issues, product problems, and experience gaps you can fix. ^[FIL-RW-006]

7. **Me-Too can evolve to Break-the-Ceiling** — by incorporating new technology or trends. Quad Lock's car mount went from Me-Too to innovative by adding wireless charging. ^[MM-RW-008]

8. **Brand name > category term = market ownership** — when "Quad Lock" gets more search volume than "iPhone bike mount", you own the market. That's the moat. ^[MM-RW-009]

9. **Distribution subsidizes marketing for margin** — using distributors just substitutes marketing dollars for margin. Better to spend on marketing because you also build brand awareness. ^[MM-RW-010]

10. **Pop-ups are conversion safety nets** — people on mobile may be interested but not ready to buy (on a train, wallet not available). Pop-ups capture emails for later conversion. ^[MM-RW-011]

---

## PADROES DECISORIOS

| Situacao | Decisao | Fonte |
|----------|---------|-------|
| Want to enter new market | Look for market pull signals first, don't go on gut | ^[MM-RW-001] |
| New product idea | Classify: Me-Too or Break-the-Ceiling? Onboarding or Complementary? | ^[MM-RW-005, MM-RW-006] |
| Product pricing | Calculate dollar margin, ensure room for CAC at scale | ^[FIL-RW-005] |
| Market validation needed | Run focused test with existing product on new landing page | ^[HEUR-RW-003] |
| First product vs second product | Master first product completely before second | ^[FIL-RW-007] |
| Channel strategy | DTC first, distribution only for underserved/geographic gaps | ^[FIL-RW-011] |
| Negative reviews appearing | Mine for actionable intelligence, don't dismiss | ^[FIL-RW-006] |
| Launch pricing | Start low, raise over time — never start high and discount | ^[FIL-RW-009] |
